pi_items.metadata_value: ["New Moon", "Waxing Crescent", "First Quarter", "Waxing Gibbous", "Full Moon", "Waning Gibbous", "Third Quarter", "Waning Crescent"]
pi_items.verified_value: new Moon, waxing crescent, first quarter, waxing gibbous, full Moon, waning gibbous, third quarter, waning crescent (exact same phases in correct order)
pi_items.source: NASA Science, timeanddate.com, and multiple authoritative astronomy sources confirm these are the eight lunar phases in correct order

pi_items.metadata_value: New Moon → Waxing Crescent → First Quarter → Waxing Gibbous → Full Moon → Waning Gibbous → Third Quarter → Waning Crescent
pi_items.verified_value: new Moon → waxing crescent → first quarter → waxing gibbous → full Moon → waning gibbous → third quarter → waning crescent (identical)
pi_items.source: NASA Science (https://science.nasa.gov/moon/moon-phases/), timeanddate.com, University of Iowa Imaging the Universe

pi_items.metadata_value: 29 or 29.5 days (as mentioned in task description: 'approximately 29.5 days')
pi_items.verified_value: 29.5 days (more precisely: 29 days, 12 hours, 44 minutes, and 3 seconds)
pi_items.source: NASA Science, timeanddate.com, ScienceDirect, US Naval Observatory (https://aa.usno.navy.mil/faq/moon_phases)

pi_items.metadata_value: Synodic period (time between successive new moons)
pi_items.verified_value: Synodic period of the Moon (also called lunar month). Not to be confused with sidereal period (27.3 days, the Moon's orbital period around Earth)
pi_items.source: NASA Science, timeanddate.com clarify the distinction between synodic period (29.5 days) and sidereal period (27.3 days)

pi_items.verified_value: Moon Phases is the standard astronomical terminology
pi_items.source: NASA Science and authoritative astronomy sources

pi_items.verified_value: 29.5 days is the scientifically precise value for the lunar cycle
pi_items.source: NASA Science, timeanddate.com, US Naval Observatory

pi_items.verified_value: Essential term - the Sun's position relative to Earth and Moon determines all lunar phases
pi_items.source: NASA Science (https://science.nasa.gov/moon/moon-phases/) explains phases result from Sun-Earth-Moon geometry

pi_items.verified_value: Essential term - the Moon orbits Earth, and moon phases are observed from Earth looking at the Moon illuminated by the Sun
pi_items.source: NASA Science explains the Sun-Earth-Moon relationship drives all lunar phases
These are task design requirements for student engagement, not scientific facts

privileged_info_summary: The moon phases diagram task is based on accurate astronomical facts. The eight moon phases are correctly named and ordered: New Moon, Waxing Crescent, First Quarter, Waxing Gibbous, Full Moon, Waning Gibbous, Third Quarter, and Waning Crescent. These phases repeat on a lunar cycle of approximately 29.5 days (more precisely 29 days, 12 hours, 44 minutes, and 3 seconds), known as the synodic period. Moon phases result from the Sun-Earth-Moon orbital geometry, making the inclusion of 'Sun' and 'Earth' in explanations scientifically essential. All eight phases are equally valid and commonly taught in earth science curricula.
